demand.source: pending IEM Part A minus Part B 2020-2025

supply_ledger_acres.note: national, all 37 states, vacant plots only (indiaindustriallandbank.gov.in)

ONLY Odisha IDCO quantified (Category B); other states hold undeveloped banks the central portal misses -- Odisha proved the central figure understated ~20x, so national total is FAR higher but unquantified
supply_ledger_acres.quantified: Odisha only -- national is a large LOWER BOUND

supply_ledger_acres.note: Gujarat ~1,800 ha since Jan-2024; UPSIDA/MIDC/RIICO parallel drives unquantified
~3,400 ac CPSE surplus referred to DIPAM (Economic Survey 2021-22)
1,436 ongoing liquidations + continuous SARFAESI auctions; IBBI publishes COUNTS not acreage, so no aggregate area -- built parcel-by-parcel from auction notices

~378,651 sq km OALP licence area -- a RIGHT TO EXPLORE within a polygon, not usable industrial land; 90% offshore; well-pad footprint tiny. EXCLUDED from siteable supply. Re-use pool = depleted onshore-field SURFACE land, unsized publicly

headline: Whether India has enough industrial land for its pending pipeline depends ENTIRELY on the land-intensity of that pipeline. Heavy-industry pipeline -> large deficit; light/efficient -> surplus. The pending pipeline is metals/chemicals-heavy (see sector data), pushing toward the land-intensive end.
the_swing_factor: The Odisha finding is decisive: the central IILB understated one state's developable land ~20x (5,162 -> 109,000+ acres once its undeveloped IDCO bank counted). If that ratio holds even partially nationally, the TRUE developable supply dwarfs the quantified 365,601 acres and would cover even the land-intensive scenario -- but almost all of it is UNDEVELOPED (needs roads/power/water) and UNQUANTIFIED (only Odisha has a machine-readable land bank). The binding constraint is not raw land, it is DEVELOPED, SITED, ready-to-build land -- of which only ~253,725 acres (IILB) exists nationally.
caveats: Demand is Rs cr converted to acres via a land-intensity assumption -- the scenarios ARE the uncertainty, not a hidden one.

Most supply layers are partial: only Odisha's undeveloped bank, Gujarat's resumed plots and IILB's developed land are fully quantified; IBC/SARFAESI is count-only; other states' undeveloped banks are unquantified lower bounds.
Petroleum exploration acreage (93.6M acres) is EXCLUDED from siteable supply -- it is licence area, not usable land.
Pending IEM approximates the unconverted backlog, not a clean stock.
